title: Resistance War Against the French (1945-1954)
summary: To protect the newly gained independence, our entire nation entered a long-term resistance war against the return of the French colonialists.

slides.text: Not wanting us to be free, the French colonialists sent their troops to invade our country once again. Uncle Ho called out: 'We would rather sacrifice everything than lose our country'.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese people from all walks of life gathering under a banner 'QUYET TU CHO TO QUOC QUYET SINH', determined faces, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The whole country entered a arduous resistance. Families had to leave the cities for the resistance bases (mountainous regions) to protect our forces.
slides.imagePrompt: A family traveling through mountain pass at night with torches, carrying simple belongings, moonlit forest, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Despite the lack of food and clothes, our army and people remained optimistic, producing food together and fighting bravely everywhere.
slides.imagePrompt: Soldiers and peasants planting corn and rice together in a forest clearing, singing and working, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Resounding victories like the Viet Bac and Border campaigns made the French troops falter, affirming the power of people's warfare.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese soldiers in green uniforms and pith helmets (mũ cối) using clever tactics in the jungle, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Which call from Uncle Ho inspired the people to stand up against the French?
What did our people do to support the resistance?
options: Stayed in the city for fun
Went to resistance bases to produce and fight
slides.answer: Went to resistance bases to produce and fight
